name: Red Pepper Pork and Noodles
recipeIngredient: 5 oz Rice sticks or noodles - angel hair pasta, or vermicelli
2 tbsp Reduced-sodium soy sauce or oyster sauce
2 tbsp Finely chopped fresh lemongrass or 3/4 tsp. finely shredded lemon peel
1 medium Red, yellow, orange, or green sweet pepper, cut into thin strips
2 oz Pea pods, halved crosswise diagonally (1 cup)
1 medium Orange, peeled, cut crosswise into 1/2-inch thick slices, and halved
recipeInstructions:
1. Trim fat from pork. Partially freeze meat. Thinly slice across the grain into bite-size strips. Set aside.
2. For sauce, in a small mixing bowl stir together broth, 2 tablespoons soy sauce, and cornstarch; set aside.
3. Soak rice sticks for 15 minutes in enough warm water to cover. Drain well. Cut into 2- to 3-inch lengths, if desired. Set aside. (Or, break pasta into 2- to 3-inch lengths; cook according to package directions.)
4. In another mixing bowl combine pork strips, 2 tablespoons soy sauce or oyster sauce, lemongrass or lemon peel, sesame oil, and crushed red pepper. Cover and let stand for 20 minutes.
5. Pour cooking oil into a wok or 12-inch skillet. Add more oil as necessary during cooking. Preheat over medium-high heat. Stir-fry garlic in hot oil for 15 seconds. Add green onion, pepper strips, and pea pods; stir fry for 2 minutes or till vegetables are crisp-tender. Remove vegetables from wok.
6. Add meat mixture to the hot wok. Stir-fry for 2 to 3 minutes or until no pink remains. Push meat to sides of wok. Stir sauce; add to center of wok. Cook and stir till thickened and bubbly. Add soaked rice sticks or cooked pasta and vegetables; stir to coat. Stir in orange slices. Serve immediately. If desired, garnish with orange wedges and green onions.
7. Makes 4 main-dish servings.
